metadata.title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI Management System Standard — Complete Annex A Controls
metadata.remarks: Community OSCAL catalog for ISO/IEC 42001:2023. Covers all 38 controls in Annex A (A.2 through A.10) across 9 control objective groups. Source: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 standard with reference to https://orbit.reconn.io/iso-42001-controls-guide/. Controls with behavioral fixture evidence noted in guidance sections.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish an AI policy informed by business strategy, organizational values, risk levels, legal requirements, and risk environment. The AI policy shall include principles guiding AI activity and processes for handling exceptions.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Procedural control: AI policy document required. Behavioral evidence: fixture cases verify AI system behavior conforms to stated policy principles (e.g., safety, fairness, transparency).

controls.title: A.2.3 — Alignment with Other Organisational Policies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall analyze intersections between AI activities and existing policies (quality, security, privacy, safety, ethics) with updates ensuring consistency across the organization.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Procedural control: policy alignment documentation required. Behavioral evidence: fixture suite spans multiple policy domains (security, privacy, safety) to verify consistent behavior across policy boundaries.

controls.title: A.2.4 — Review of the AI Policy

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A named, management-approved role shall own periodic reviews of the AI policy, triggered by material organizational, strategic, legal, or technological changes, with documented review cycles and records.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Procedural control: review records required. Behavioral evidence: fixture suite re-run after policy changes; drift index detecting behavioral deviations from updated policy.

controls.title: A.3.2 — Roles and Responsibilities Related to AI

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall explicitly define and communicate AI-specific roles, accountability structures for system risk, review authority, escalation pathways, and halt authority.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Procedural control: RACI or similar documentation required. Behavioral evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system correctly escalates to the appropriate role when authority boundaries are reached.

controls.title: A.3.3 — Reporting of Concerns Regarding AI Systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish a formal mechanism enabling personnel to raise AI concerns without retaliation, distinct from general event logging.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Procedural control: concern-reporting channel required. Behavioral evidence: fixture cases verify AI system encourages reporting of problematic outputs and does not suppress concerns.

groups.title: A.4 — Resources for AI Systems (6 controls)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall maintain a current inventory of AI systems within scope, updated as systems are deployed, modified, or decommissioned.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture metadata (skill_or_agent, bundle_id, bundle_version, certification_track) constitutes a machine-readable AI system inventory entry.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall document the resources each AI system uses: components, data, tooling, computing infrastructure, and human expertise required for its operation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: environment_fingerprint.json in evidence tarball documents OS, arch, agentcarousel version, git SHA; run summary documents generator_model, tokens, and cost.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall document data resources covering provenance, update dates, categories, labelling processes, intended use, quality, retention, known bias issues, and preparation steps.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: data_handling field (synthetic-only, no-pii, pii-reviewed) and fixture case metadata document data resource characteristics; REDACTION_POLICY.md in evidence tarball.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall document algorithmic types, ML models, data conditioning tools, evaluation methods, and development and deployment tooling.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: run summary documents generator_model, judge_model, and evaluator type; evaluator_config in fixture YAML documents evaluation tooling.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall document hardware, hosting environment, processing and storage resources, and environmental impact.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: environment_fingerprint.json in evidence tarball documents OS, architecture, and execution environment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall document that required expertise is available for development, deployment, and operation of AI systems, including diverse demographic representation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Procedural control: staffing documentation required. Behavioral evidence: diverse authorship of fixture cases and rubric dimensions.

groups.title: A.5 — Assessing Impacts of AI Systems (3 controls)

controls.title: A.5.2 — AI System Impact Assessment Process

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish a repeatable, documented methodology for assessing potential impacts of the AI system on individuals, groups, and society, applied consistently across systems.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: behavioral coverage completeness score across 7-category risk taxonomy provides a repeatable impact assessment methodology.

controls.title: A.5.3 — Internal Impact Assessment of AI Systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall conduct impact assessments examining discriminatory outcomes, privacy violations, safety risks, and societal effects, with results feeding into deployment decisions.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases covering bias, privacy, and safety scenarios; effectiveness scores used in deployment (certification_track promotion) decisions.

controls.title: A.5.4 — Functionality and Behaviour of the AI System

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall ensure systems function as intended with deviation detection and remediation mechanisms during operation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: behavioral drift index detecting functional deviations; regression gate in agc compare; behavioral stability score.

groups.title: A.6 — AI System Life Cycle (10 controls)

controls.title: A.6.1.1 — Design of the AI System

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Requirements and design specifications shall be documented before development, reflecting intended use case, identified risks, and governance constraints.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture YAML schema_version, skill_or_agent, and case descriptions document design specifications; risk_tier and certification_track reflect governance constraints.

controls.title: A.6.1.2 — Data for Development and Enhancement

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall govern development data covering privacy and security, data-dependent threats, transparency and explainability, training data representativeness, and accuracy and integrity.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: data_handling field; synthetic-only fixtures verifying system behavior without real PII; bias-detection test cases.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Documentation shall be maintained throughout development capturing design decisions, testing, validation, and changes creating audit trails.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: git history of fixture changes; signed evidence tarballs with run ID, started_at, finished_at, and config_hash provide immutable audit trail.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall implement explicit controls for identifying, assessing, and addressing bias in training and operational data.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases testing diverse demographic inputs; rubric dimensions for fairness and non-discrimination; bias-tagged test coverage.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall test that AI systems maintain intended performance under adversarial conditions, edge cases, and unexpected inputs.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: injection resistance score; adversarial fixture pass rate; edge-case coverage in behavioral taxonomy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall document the operational deployment specifying users, use cases, interfaces, and constraints defining correct operation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: skill_or_agent metadata; fixture case descriptions defining intended users and use cases; system prompt documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall conduct structured pre-deployment testing verifying specified performance with documented scope, methodology, data, results, and sign-off.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: full fixture-based evaluation (agc eval) with run JSON documenting methodology, results, and version; certification_track promotion constitutes sign-off.

controls.title: A.6.2.3 — Human Oversight of AI Systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Reviewers shall have genuine override authority; AI system output accuracy and consistency shall be monitored; functioning concern-reporting mechanisms shall be maintained; and oversight appropriateness shall be assessed for each use case.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: escalation latency (p50/p95 ms); escalation precision/recall; fixture cases verifying override signals are clear and actionable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall log AI system use (time, date, production data, out-of-range outputs) retained per intended use and legal requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: execution trace in CaseResult (steps, final_output, latency_ms, tokens); run JSON with started_at, finished_at; history DB persistence.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall maintain a documented deployment plan verifying all requirements met before live operation, including specifications, testing, impact assessments, oversight, and documentation completion.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: certification_track: trusted status represents deployment readiness; evidence tarball constitutes deployment gate documentation.

groups.title: A.7 — Data for AI Systems (4 controls)

controls.title: A.7.2 — Data for Development and Enhancement of AI System

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall manage operational data covering privacy and security, data-dependent threats, transparency and explainability, training and operational representativeness, and accuracy and integrity.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: data_handling classification; PHI leakage rate; fixture cases testing data representativeness across diverse scenarios.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall govern data sourcing covering categories, quantities, sources, source characteristics, subject demographics, prior handling, data rights, labelling metadata, and provenance.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: bundle_id and bundle_version in fixture metadata provide data provenance; data_handling field documents acquisition category.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall define quality criteria (accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness) and maintain processes detecting and remediating quality issues throughout the system lifecycle.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture validation (agc validate) enforces schema quality; effectiveness score measures output accuracy quality; calibration score tracks score consistency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall implement controls for appropriate personal data handling within AI systems connecting to existing privacy frameworks.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: PHI leakage rate; data_handling: synthetic-only or no-pii classification; REDACTION_POLICY.md in evidence tarball.

groups.title: A.8 — Information for Interested Parties (5 controls)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall make available information about system characteristics including intended use, capabilities, limitations, and conditions for non-use.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture case descriptions document system characteristics; Certification Scope and Limitations in evidence export explicitly discloses capabilities and limitations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Appropriate disclosure shall be made when individuals interact with or are subject to AI system decisions, context-dependent per jurisdiction.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases testing whether AI system discloses its AI nature; transparency rubric dimensions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall inform users and affected parties of known limitations, accuracy degradation conditions, edge cases, and identified biases.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: confidence calibration score; rubric dimensions testing whether AI system appropriately qualifies its outputs and discloses uncertainty.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall clearly communicate system design purposes and non-purposes, preventing out-of-scope use.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: negative fixture cases verifying AI system correctly refuses out-of-scope requests and communicates scope boundaries.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall maintain processes for communicating material system changes to affected parties upon updates, retraining, or intended use modifications.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: bundle_version versioning in fixture metadata tracks system changes; re-attestation workflow documents model updates.

groups.title: A.9 — Responsible Use of AI Systems (3 controls)

controls.title: A.9.2 — Intended Use of AI Systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall implement operational safeguards, user training, and monitoring mechanisms ensuring systems operate only within intended purposes.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: negative and out-of-scope fixture cases verifying behavioral limits; injection resistance ensuring adversarial prompt attempts to expand scope are blocked.

controls.title: A.9.3 — Responsibilities and Obligations for Appropriate Use

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall clearly document responsibilities for appropriate use including contractual terms, user agreements, and internal accountability structures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: evidence tarball Certification Scope and Limitations section documents use obligations and re-attestation responsibilities.

controls.title: A.9.4 — Addressing Misuse of AI Systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall implement mechanisms for detecting, responding to, and learning from misuse instances by internal actors, external users, or adversarial parties.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: prompt injection resistance score; adversarial fixture pass rate; agc audit root-cause classification of misuse-driven failures.

groups.title: A.10 — Third-Party and Customer Relationships (2 controls)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall govern external AI relationships covering due diligence, contractual requirements, and ongoing oversight of sourced components, models, or services.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: bundle trust-check verifying third-party fixture bundle attestation; generator_model field tracking third-party model provenance; bundle manifest SHA256 hashes ensuring supply chain integrity.

controls.title: A.10.3 — Responsibilities Along the AI Life Cycle

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall clearly allocate responsibilities where multiple organizations share AI system lifecycle responsibilities across development, deployment, and operation stages.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: certification_track and bundle_id in fixture metadata attribute lifecycle responsibility; evidence tarball records which organization ran the attestation at which stage.
